items.title: Author Correction: Brain tumors induce widespread disruption of calvarial bone and alteration of skull marrow immune landscape
items.author: Jinan Behnan
items.content: Author Correction: Brain tumors induce widespread disruption of calvarial bone and alteration of skull marrow immune landscape

items.title: Genetic targeting of premyelinating oligodendrocytes reveals activity-dependent myelination mechanisms
items.author: Lu O. Sun
items.content: A new in vivo genetic tool allows labeling, tracing and manipulation of premyelinating oligodendrocytes, enabling further investigations of this transient cell stage during myelination.

items.title: A genome-wide analysis of the shared genetic risk architecture of complex neurological and psychiatric disorders
items.author: Ole A. Andreassen
items.content: Smeland et al. demonstrate greater genetic overlap between neurological and psychiatric disorders than previously recognized, along with diverse neurobiological associations. The findings support a more integrated view of brain-related illness.

items.title: Estrogen modulates reward prediction errors and reinforcement learning
items.author: Christine M. Constantinople
items.content: Dopamine encoding of reward prediction errors naturally fluctuates over females’ reproductive cycles with estrogenic signaling due to reduced expression of dopamine reuptake proteins.

items.title: APOE4 to APOE2 allelic switching in mice improves Alzheimer’s disease-related metabolic signatures, neuropathology and cognition
items.author: Lance A. Johnson
items.content: Using a mouse model enabling an inducible ‘switch’, Golden et al. show that an astrocyte-specific replacement of the Alzheimer’s risk gene APOE4 with protective APOE2 alters metabolism and gene expression, reducing amyloid pathology and gliosis.

items.title: High estrogen states enhance reinforcement learning
items.author: Melissa J. Sharpe
items.content: We have all heard the cliché; hormones make us unreliable and unpredictable. However, in this issue of Nature Neuroscience, Golden et al. show that higher states of endogenous estrogen enhance reward prediction errors in sophisticated ways, which promotes adaptive behavior and improves performance.

items.title: Flipping APOE to reverse Alzheimer’s disease traits
items.author: Guojun Bu
items.content: In this issue of Nature Neuroscience, Golden et al. unveil a mouse model that enables in vivo ‘switching’ from the Alzheimer’s risk gene APOE4 to the protective gene APOE2. This genetic conversion reshapes Alzheimer’s disease-related metabolism, reduces neuropathology, and enhances cognition, demonstrating the beneficial effects of APOE genetic targeting as a promising therapeutic strategy.

items.title: Author Correction: Transsynaptic labeling and transcriptional control of zebrafish neural circuits
items.author: Marnie E. Halpern
items.content: Author Correction: Transsynaptic labeling and transcriptional control of zebrafish neural circuits
